title: microlepia marginalis var. calvescens
intro: Microlepia marginalis var. calvescens, also known as Paddy's Ginger or Dandelion of the forest, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Microlepia marginalis var. calvescens include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as being used as a diuretic and to promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically in the form of poultices or compresses.
